slides.title: Actions and Activities
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 3 English, Unit 5: Actions and Activities. Welcome to Unit 5! This unit is all about actions and activities — the things people DO. You will learn lots of action words (verbs) such as build, discuss, protect and celebrate. You will talk about things you did, using the past simple, and learn to explain why things happened using words like because and so. You will put events in order with First, Next, Then and Finally, ask Wh- questions, and share your ideas politely. By the end, you will be ready to read this unit's story, “The Wall Behind the Garden”, where a class of children work together to build something special. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name and use many action words, such as build, listen, protect and celebrate
Talk about things you did, using the past simple (built, listened, celebrated)
Explain why something happened, using because and so
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name and use many action words, such as build, listen, protect and celebrate. you will also talk about things you did, using the past simple (built, listened, celebrated). you will also explain why something happened, using because and so. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: discuss, listen, offer, build, complete, remove. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes A Busy Saturday and Helping Hands. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: The Past Simple Tense
bullets: We use the past simple to talk about actions that already happened and are finished. It helps us tell stories and share things we did. In this unit's...
slides.narration: The first language focus is The Past Simple Tense. We use the past simple to talk about actions that already happened and are finished. It helps us tell stories and share things we did. In this unit's story, the children built a wall — that already happened, so we say built, not build. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 — Tell What You Did. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
